trajectory.model_output:
<python_code>
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Step 1: Open the gripper before grasping
open_gripper()

# Step 2: Get the grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close the gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut and the peg (white hollow center) to compute relative transform
nut_center_position, nut_center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')
peg_position, peg_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')  # Assuming this gives the peg location/orientation

# Compute the fixed offset from nut center to handle in world frame at time of grasp
# This defines how the handle is positioned relative to the nut center
offset_translation = nut_center_position - handle_position

# Also compute the orientation difference (this will help maintain correct alignment during insertion)
handle_rotation = R.from_quat(handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # WXYZ -> scalar last internally
nut_center_rotation = R.from_quat(nut_center_quaternion_wxyz)
relative_rotation = nut_center_rotation * handle_rotation.inv()  # How nut orients relative to handle

# Step 5: Move above the peg with correct orientation for insertion
insertion_position = peg_position + np.array([0, 0, 0.1])  # 10cm above peg
target_orientation = (relative_rotation * handle_rotation).as_quat()  # Desired final nut orientation

goto_pose(insertion_position, target_orientation, z_approach=0.0)  # Already above, so no extra approach needed

# Step 6: Perform precise insertion motion
final_insertion_pose = peg_position  # At the same level as the peg
goto_pose(final_insertion_pose, target_orientation, z_approach=0.0)

# Step 7: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 8: Retract slightly and go home
retract_position = final_insertion_pose + np.array([0, 0, 0.1])
goto_pose(retract_position, target_orientation)
goto_home_joint_position()
</python_code>

import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R

# Step 1: Open the gripper before grasping
open_gripper()

# Step 2: Get the grasp pose for the extruded handle of the brown square nut
handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz = sample_grasp_pose('extruded handle of the brown square nut')

# Step 3: Approach and grasp the handle
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz, z_approach=0.1)  # Approach from 10cm above
goto_pose(handle_position, handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # Move to actual grasp pose
close_gripper()  # Close the gripper to grasp the nut

# Step 4: Get the center of the brown square nut and the peg (white hollow center) to compute relative transform
nut_center_position, nut_center_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('white hollow center of the brown square nut')
peg_position, peg_quaternion_wxyz = get_object_pose('brown square block')  # Assuming this gives the peg location/orientation

# Compute the fixed offset from nut center to handle in world frame at time of grasp
# This defines how the handle is positioned relative to the nut center
offset_translation = nut_center_position - handle_position

# Also compute the orientation difference (this will help maintain correct alignment during insertion)
handle_rotation = R.from_quat(handle_quaternion_wxyz)  # WXYZ -> scalar last internally
nut_center_rotation = R.from_quat(nut_center_quaternion_wxyz)
relative_rotation = nut_center_rotation * handle_rotation.inv()  # How nut orients relative to handle

# Step 5: Move above the peg with correct orientation for insertion
insertion_position = peg_position + np.array([0, 0, 0.1])  # 10cm above peg
target_orientation = (relative_rotation * handle_rotation).as_quat()  # Desired final nut orientation

goto_pose(insertion_position, target_orientation, z_approach=0.0)  # Already above, so no extra approach needed

# Step 6: Perform precise insertion motion
final_insertion_pose = peg_position  # At the same level as the peg
goto_pose(final_insertion_pose, target_orientation, z_approach=0.0)

# Step 7: Release the nut
open_gripper()

# Step 8: Retract slightly and go home
retract_position = final_insertion_pose + np.array([0, 0, 0.1])
goto_pose(retract_position, target_orientation)
goto_home_joint_position()
